chief_complaint: A 75-year-old woman presents with fever, cough, and dyspnea.
history: The patient has late-onset, seropositive rheumatoid arthritis and started treatment with methotrexate and prednisolone two weeks prior to presentation. She developed symptoms of fever, cough, and dyspnea two weeks after starting methotrexate.
exam: Vital signs: fever, cough, and dyspnea. Physical examination findings: bilateral pulmonary involvement.
labs: Lung ultrasound and chest computed tomography revealed bilateral pulmonary involvement. Methotrexate was discontinued and the corticosteroid dose was increased.
